meaning: n.液体,汁,酒精饮料,[药]溶液vt.浸水vi.喝酒
examples: She drinks wine and beer but no liquor.
The room was filled with cases of liquor.
In 1979, Liquor Barn thrived as a discount merchandiser.
The factory's latticed windows and ornate working liquor distillery make it worth the trip.
But all your daddy's life, on a weekend, there was liquor there in that house.
